title: The Wars in Ukraine and Iran Are More Alike Than You May Think
summary: Aspects such as drone technology and diplomacy show how the wars intersect on the battlefield and in global alignments, providing a model for future conflicts.

title: Iran Begins Lifting Monthslong Internet Blackout, Officials Say
summary: Organizations that track online traffic confirmed an uptick in activity on Tuesday, as Iranians reconnected to the global internet after a government-imposed shutdown.

title: U.S. Aims to Give Cold War Plutonium to Start-Ups for Nuclear Fuel
summary: Companies say it’s a better way to dispose of the Cold War-era material — and fix a shortage of nuclear fuel. But the plan has also faced criticism from nonproliferation experts.

title: UFC Unveils Plans for White House Fight on Trump’s Birthday
summary: The Ultimate Fighting Championship bout is scheduled for June 14, President Trump’s 80th birthday.

title: As Trump Politicizes Justice Dept., Prosecutors Struggle With Grand Juries
summary: Judges and grand juries have increasingly lost faith in the Justice Department as the president uses it to reward his friends and go after his opponents.

title: Trump Declares Himself in Good Health After Physical Exam at Walter Reed
summary: President Trump, who will turn 80 next month, did not give details on the exam.

title: At the A.I. Epicenter, Technologists Dismiss Pope Leo’s Warnings About the New Technology
summary: Pope Leo XIV’s spiritual message on artificial intelligence arrived as Silicon Valley’s A.I. enthusiasts pursue their own spirituality through technology.

title: To Understand Pope Leo’s Efforts on A.I., Look at the Man Shaking His Hand
summary: The co-founder of a prominent A.I. company was nearby as the pope implored A.I. leaders to slow down and consider the technology’s possible perils.

title: Scientists Ditched a Scary Climate Scenario. What Now?
summary: While global warming is still a threat, the decision to back away from a worst-case outlook raises questions about whether some risks have been overstated.

title: A Spring Heat Wave is Breaking Records in the U.K., Spain and France
summary: Authorities in Britain and France warned that “unprecedented” temperatures — at a time when few expected them — could persist. Climate change has made heat waves more frequent and more intense.

title: The Homesteading Mother of 6 Taking On Big Tech
summary: Kassi Solberg has concerns about a proposed data center complex, the size of 3,800 football fields, near her home. Trust us, the developer says.

title: South Carolina Redistricting: Senate Passes on New Map, Defying Trump
summary: With early voting already underway, it became clear there was no longer enough support to use new lines before the November midterm elections.

title: Court Rejects Alabama House Map, Calling It Unfair to Black Voters
summary: Alabama is likely to appeal the ruling, which stops an effort to use a new congressional map that would likely cost Democrats a majority-Black district.

title: G.D.P. Is a Flawed Measure of Prosperity. Alternatives Are On the Way.
summary: The United Nations has a new plan for weighing economic gains alongside health and environmental progress. But consensus is elusive.

title: Supreme Court Reverses Ruling in Immigration Judges’ Free Speech Lawsuit
summary: A group of immigration judges in 2020 challenged work-related restrictions on their public speaking engagements, saying they violated their free speech rights.

title: Supreme Court Rejects Lawsuit Over Commercial Driver’s Licenses for Migrants
summary: Florida officials had asked the justices to hear a lawsuit accusing California and Washington of improperly granting licenses to an immigrant accused in a fatal crash.

title: Knicks Fans Celebrate the Team’s First NBA Finals Appearance Since 1999
summary: After a 27-year drought, the New York Knicks are headed to the N.B.A. finals. Fans flooded the streets of the city in celebration.

title: ‘Bing Bong!’ The Knicks’ Watch Party Outside the Garden Is Dead.
summary: The police say that celebrations outside of Madison Square Garden have gotten out of control. Fans and insiders say the chaos has been driven by social media influencers.

title: SpaceX IPO Filing Reveals Favorable Terms for Elon Musk
summary: The ways it set up its board and Mr. Musk’s pay appear to benefit him at the expense of other shareholders, corporate governance experts said.

title: Canada Strikes Landmark Deal to Export Liquefied Natural Gas to Germany
summary: The agreement is important for both nations, as Canada seeks new markets away from the United States and Germany tries to diversify its energy supply.

title: The Average Guys Outsmarting Wall Street on Prediction Markets
summary: How prediction-market ‘sharps’ have made millions wagering on everything from war to Rotten Tomatoes.

title: They’ve Heard the Warnings. Gen Z Is Tanning Anyway.
summary: Dermatologists are flummoxed.

title: Deal or No Deal With Iran
summary: A deal to end the current blockade is merely an enticement for the next blockade and the one after that.

title: Deaths Are Reported After Chemical Tank Ruptures at Washington State Paper Mill
summary: An unknown number of injuries were also reported at the Nippon Dynawave Packaging Company in Longview, Wash., the authorities said.

title: A Powerful H.I.V. Drug Lands in Zambia. But Will It Reach Those Who Need It?
summary: Lenacapavir, which protects people from H.I.V. infection with twice-yearly shots, is arriving in a country where the health system has been hollowed out by American aid cuts.

title: BP Ousts Chairman, Citing ‘Serious Concerns’ Over Conduct
summary: The British oil group said that Albert Manifold had been removed, after concerns were raised “related to important governance standards, oversight and conduct.”
